hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s.

context.street_character: One-way EB | 40 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks | Mixed commercial-residential

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: red-brown brick with dark trim, black-painted porch details, and arched brick entry surround
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade remains striking and largely intact, though bins and graffiti on the adjacent wall slightly detract from the presentation.
photo_observations.window_details: Narrow vertically proportioned windows rise through the steep central gable, with darker replacement frames set in decorative brick openings.
photo_observations.door_details: Dark front door under an ornate black-painted porch canopy and a large arched brick surround.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Decorative gable brackets, carved trim, and porch ironwork remain visible.
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Facade reads more as a narrow gabled composition than a broad projecting bay from this frontal view.
photo_observations.porch_details: Narrow front porch with decorative black posts and canopy elements.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney visible in the cropped photo.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Diamond brickwork and the dramatic steep gable make this one of the more ornate houses in the row.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Replacement windows and dark-painted trim update the house, but the ornate massing and brick detailing remain strong.
photo_observations.notes: Best frontal view of the narrow ornate gabled house.

notes: Best frontal view of the narrow ornate gabled house.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, converted house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include original windows, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s.

facade_description: Converted semi-detached house-form (pre-1889) in red-brown brick with dark trim and an arched brick entry surround. Two storeys under a steep cross-gable, with decorative lintels, diamond brickwork, and a narrow ornate porch with black-painted posts. Subdivided into 3 dwelling units. Party walls on both sides. One of the more ornate facades in the Nassau row.
